equivalence_argument: No complete mapping was found: the verifier does process delivery_spc_calls using the origin transaction job key and checks proof length/signature data, but it does not enforce the required at-most-one delivery SPC call cardinality.

checked_queries: delivery_spc_calls\(\)|delivery_spc_calls|more than one|at most one|len\(\) > 1|SPC proof too short|missing matching delivery
negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing verifier enforcement that a delivery settlement contains at most one delivery_spc_call; extra calls can be ignored if one matching valid call is present.
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/ethereum/payload/src/ritual_async.rs constructs delivery settlements with exactly one delivery_spc_call in the builder happy path.
crates/ritual-block-verification/src/lib.rs validate_ritual_async_block_structure only requires any delivery_spc_call with a supported address and non-empty output/proof for deliver settlements.

crates/ritual-block-verification/src/lib.rs verify_spc_calls uses calls.iter().find(...) to select a matching delivery SPC call, checks proof length >= 65, and verifies signature with job.tx_hash keyed by settlement.origin_tx.
Searches found no verifier-side check rejecting multiple delivery_spc_calls or extra non-matching delivery SPC calls.

rationale: The verifier removes the previous comment that delivery_spc_calls were not verified through SPC, adds structural checks and job-key selection using tx.origin_tx_hash(), and pushes the delivery SPC call into spc_data for verify_signature_core. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: During block verification, delivery_spc_calls are processed by the same SPC signature verification pipeline as Phase 1 SPC calls, must have origin_tx_hash present, must contain at most one delivery SPC call, must target an accepted async precompile, must have a proof of at least SPC_PROOF_SIGNATURE_LENGTH, and use origin_tx_hash rather than the settlement transaction hash as the job key.
suggested_falsification_tests: Construct delivery settlement transactions with no origin_tx_hash, multiple delivery_spc_calls, too-short proof, zero executor, unsupported precompile, and a valid signed proof; assert verifier rejects each malformed case and accepts the valid case only when the proof verifies against the original Phase 1 tx hash.
summary: Delivery SPC verification is mostly present but lacks the required at-most-one call rejection.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Create a delivery settlement containing one valid matching delivery_spc_call plus a second extra call; current verifier should find and verify the matching call instead of rejecting the transaction for cardinality.
title: Delivery SPC calls are verified using original transaction hash
